rules.recommendation: Size up 1–2 full grades from nominal (L → XL/XXL).
rules.rationale: BBS block patterns run exceptionally narrow; object-dyed shirts marketed slim. A 44" measured chest does not fit the nominal grade.

rules.recommendation: Mandatory upsize on coated denim and painter pants.
rules.rationale: Slim/straight-leg silhouettes with no mechanical tolerance at 200 lb.
Narrow block pattern across the bottoms line.
Severe architectural restriction in shoulders and high armholes; nominal chest equivalence lies on this block.
rules.source: Sizing matrix · Rick Owens leather outerwear

rules.recommendation: Size DOWN, often to M or S despite the nominal waist tag.
rules.rationale: Drop-crotch + elastic waist give massive mechanical tolerance; standard sizing produces excess fabric.
rules.source: Sizing matrix · Rick Owens drop-crotch bottoms

rules.recommendation: Size down 0.5–1 full size from nominal.
rules.rationale: Mainline Ramones/Geobaskets run a generous toe box; a US 10 D fits a 9–9.5.
rules.source: Sizing matrix · Rick Owens mainline footwear

rules.recommendation: Size down up to 2 full sizes for the compression fit.
rules.rationale: Sock sneakers are engineered for compression; nominal size swims.
rules.source: Sizing matrix · Rick Owens sock sneakers

rules.recommendation: IT 54 minimum. IT 52 only if the textile has significant lateral stretch.
rules.rationale: IT grading: 52=XL≈43.3", 54=XXL≈45.2", 56=3XL≈47.2". A 44" measured chest sits above IT 52 nominal with no margin. Stretch is load-bearing.

genericFallback.recommendation: Baseline XL / IT 52–54. Reject slim European L; reject any 'slim fit' cut.
genericFallback.rationale: 44" measured chest on a 200 lb frame sits at the L–XL boundary unless the textile stretches; err toward the roomier grade.
genericFallback.source: Your biometric baseline (no brand rule on file)
